content:
@everyone I've officially opened up my coordination repo!
https://github.com/omnichromia/morimens-volunteer-coordination
**This is an alternative, more organized way to contribute to the BIAV Localization project.**

I'll do my best to cut up and sort out the BIAV Source Sheets into manageable chunks, and that may take a while, but I'll update the repo with my progress regularly. Currently, I have the entire "**Story**" sheet neatly organized, with "Stage" & "StageGroup" keys included.

You're of course still welcome to use the BIAV repo's fork-and-pull system; I'll do my best to keep my files up-to-date with compiled pulls from the main repo. The more people use this system though, the easier it is on me, so the preference is clear lol.

## HOW TO CONTRIBUTE:

1. Make sure you have collaborator access. @ me, DM me, or open an Issue in the repo and I'll add you in!
2. Navigate to the section you want to work on and download the **MAIN** file.
3. Open the LEDGER in that folder, and write your DISCORD USERNAME on the same line as the header for the language you're contributing;
4. If that header is already taken, please work on a different file.
5. Do your thing! When you're ready, save the file as .xlsx (or UTF-8 .csv).
> Don't worry about what the file looks like. As long as it contains the unmodified KEY column, you can include or exclude anything else.
> Deleting rows is fine as well. **All I need is KEY and your contributions.**
6. **RENAME your file** and remove "MAIN" from it to avoid confusion. Other specifics don't matter.
7. Upload your file to the same exact folder you downloaded the original from, and I'll handle the rest!

embeds.title: GitHub - omnichromia/morimens-volunteer-coordination
embeds.description: Contribute to omnichromia/morimens-volunteer-coordination development by creating an account on GitHub.
